evals.prompt: How do I set up a welcome autoresponder sequence in GetResponse for new subscribers?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide to creating autoresponders in GetResponse — explaining the day-of-cycle concept, creating multiple autoresponder messages with appropriate delays, and plan considerations (autoresponders available on all plans, automation workflows require Marketer+).

assertions.name: explains_autoresponders
assertions.description: Explains that autoresponders are time-based sequences triggered by subscription date with day-of-cycle settings

assertions.name: provides_setup_steps
assertions.description: Provides concrete steps to create autoresponder messages with recommended timing (Day 0 welcome, Day 1, Day 3, etc.)

assertions.name: clarifies_terminology
assertions.description: Clarifies that contacts must be added to a 'campaign' (which is GetResponse's term for mailing list) and distinguishes autoresponders from automation workflows

evals.prompt: I want to build a webinar funnel in GetResponse that captures leads, sends reminders, and follows up after the webinar. What plan do I need and how do I set it up?
evals.expected_output: Explains that webinars require Creator plan ($69/mo) or higher, and conversion funnels require Marketer plan ($59/mo). Walks through creating a webinar funnel: registration landing page, confirmation and reminder autoresponder sequence, webinar setup (live or on-demand), and post-webinar follow-up automation.

assertions.name: identifies_plan_requirements
assertions.description: Correctly identifies that webinars need Creator plan+ and conversion funnels need Marketer plan+

assertions.name: covers_funnel_flow
assertions.description: Describes the end-to-end funnel: registration page → email reminders → webinar → follow-up sequence

assertions.name: mentions_integration
assertions.description: Explains how GetResponse webinars integrate with automation workflows for attendee vs non-attendee follow-up sequences

evals.prompt: How do I use the GetResponse API to create a contact, add them to a list, and tag them?
evals.expected_output: Provides the API endpoint, authentication header, and a complete JSON example for POST /v3/contacts that includes campaign assignment and tag assignment in a single call.

assertions.name: correct_endpoint
assertions.description: Uses POST /v3/contacts as the endpoint with X-Auth-Token header for authentication

assertions.name: includes_campaign
assertions.description: Shows that the contact must include a 'campaign' object with campaignId to assign to a list (explaining that 'campaign' = mailing list in GetResponse)

assertions.name: includes_tags
assertions.description: Shows how to include tags in the contact creation request using the tags array with tagId objects
